senses.definition: to make time for a specific activity, task, or purpose within one's schedule

examples.x: Even with a busy schedule, she managed to find time to exercise every day.
It can be challenging to find time for a family vacation when work demands are high.
Parents often struggle to find time for themselves when raising young children.
During the weekend, he tried to find time to work on his personal projects.
Balancing work and personal life requires finding time for relaxation and leisure activities.

senses.definition: to spend or use time in a way that does not achieve anything or have a particular goal

examples.x: When the meeting was delayed, they killed time by chatting with each other.
We played a few rounds of chess to kill the time before our meeting.
While waiting for the train, she decided to kill time by reading a book.

senses.definition: to adjust the time on a clock by moving it backward, often by one hour, to account for the end of daylight saving time

examples.x: On the last Sunday of daylight saving time, remember to turn the clock back by one hour to return to standard time.
I turned the clock back on my alarm clock and my car's dashboard clock to ensure I wouldn't be early for my morning appointments.
She turned the clock back in the living room before heading to bed to adjust for the end of daylight saving time.
I always turn the clock back in my bedroom when daylight saving time ends to enjoy an extra hour of sleep.

senses.definition: to adjust the time on a clock by moving it ahead, often by one hour, to accommodate the start of daylight saving time

examples.x: This Sunday, don't forget to turn the clock forward by one hour when you go to bed as daylight saving time begins.
I'll need to turn the clock forward on my wristwatch and the wall clock in the living room to match the new time setting.
It's time to turn the clock forward and spring forward for daylight saving time.
I always turn the clock forward in my bedroom before going to sleep when daylight saving time begins.

senses.definition: to adjust the time on a clock by moving it backward, typically by one hour, to account for the end of daylight saving time

examples.x: It's that time of the year when we need to move the clock back one hour to revert to standard time after daylight saving time.
I always move the clock back in my bedroom when daylight saving time ends to enjoy an extra hour of sleep.
Don't forget to move the clock back on your phone when you wake up tomorrow.

senses.definition: to adjust the time on a clock by advancing it forward, typically by one hour, to accommodate the start of daylight saving time

examples.x: Every spring, we need to move the clock ahead by one hour to ensure our timekeeping is in sync with daylight saving time.
I always forget to move the clock ahead, so I end up being an hour early for appointments when daylight saving time begins.
Let's move the clock ahead before we go to bed so we're not late tomorrow.
